methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $68M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Axelera AI, a Series B chip design company founded by Fabrizio Del Maffeo and Evangelos Eleftheriou, sits within a competitive frontier-semiconductor sector. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers (including Positron AI, Etched, and d-Matrix) and 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $68M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage progression, implying $129M. Peer-median anchoring across the 6 private comparables—ranging from $500M to $5.0B—yields a median valuation of $1.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 24.4x across the three tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private-stage companies. 

Triangulation of the two applicable methods produces a range of $110M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $565M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression suggests conservative valuation anchored to capital deployment, while peer-median reflects the elevated valuations prevalent in AI-infrastructure chip design. Resolution of this range depends on visibility into Axelera's commercial traction, competitive positioning within the peer set, and burn rate relative to capital on hand.
